findings.line: Three rowboats lay above the waterline. One had rotted through at the bow. One was tied off. The third had been shoved up in a hurry and left free.
findings.reason: Four consecutive short declaratives flatten the shoreline scan into a template inventory rhythm.
findings.suggestedFix: Three rowboats lay above the waterline: one rotted through at the bow, one tied off, one shoved up in a hurry and left free.

findings.line: A chalk-filmed slate hung over the desk. Shelves and specimen jars lined the walls. A cold cup sat on a side table. Boards bore two hands' work.
findings.reason: The sanctum scan repeats the same medium-length subject-verb opening four times in a row.
findings.suggestedFix: A chalk-filmed slate hung over the desk; shelves and specimen jars crowded the walls, and a cold cup sat beside boards worked by two hands.

findings.line: Open folios lay beside nested diagrams. Jaina's copied farmhouse pages lay beside his own work on the desk. Chalk ground into the grain. A cup gone cold beside the place where Jaina had stood.
findings.reason: Another desk-inventory paragraph reuses the same clipped listing rhythm instead of varying sentence shape.
findings.suggestedFix: Open folios and nested diagrams covered the desk beside Jaina's copied farmhouse pages, chalk ground into the wood and a cold cup left by her place.

findings.line: Lyriel's place. Still living in the motion.
findings.reason: The fragments subtitle Cyndia's involuntary rightward check after the gesture already carried the absence.
findings.suggestedFix: Lyriel's place.

findings.line: Poison in the phrasing, yes. Not enough poison to erase the fact.
findings.reason: The narration interprets Jandice's envy after the dialogue already delivered both the bitterness and the usable fact.

findings.line: Honest. His plain delivery hit harder than a sneer.
findings.reason: Kel'Thuzad's line already conveys sincerity; this sentence pair explains the impact instead of trusting the line.
findings.suggestedFix: He said it plainly.

findings.line: No sneer. No bait.
findings.reason: This is pure explanatory captioning after the compliment has already landed.

findings.line: Good. Fast. Still the same language between them.
findings.reason: The sentence trio translates the cooperative combat beat instead of letting the trap-and-shot sequence prove it.
findings.suggestedFix: They still moved in the same language.

findings.line: It worked, but she had checked before she moved.
findings.reason: The sentence summarizes the trust-delay after the action already demonstrated it.
findings.suggestedFix: It had cost a heartbeat.

findings.line: Even dying, he gave them a direction.
findings.reason: Kel'Thuzad's quoted line already gives the direction; this sentence translates the obvious.

findings.line: Here, the chalk mattered.
findings.reason: The narration steps in to explain the significance of the chalk instead of leaving the desk evidence to do it.

findings.line: Even dead, Kel'Thuzad left the wards cycling. Still foul. Still active. Without Kel'Thuzad at the center, the wards were already slipping their pattern.
findings.reason: The paragraph opens with a thesis about ward-state, elaborates it, then summarizes it instead of staying inside the failing mechanics.
findings.suggestedFix: Even dead, Kel'Thuzad left the wards cycling; fungi-light dimmed at the wrong moments, doors stood partway open, and a bell above gave one uncertain note.

findings.line: The clean room below would sit over the same butcher's work.
findings.reason: The paragraph stops on an interpretive cap instead of ending on the jaw-in-the-bucket image that already carried the point.
findings.suggestedFix: The clean room below would be built over the same butcher's work.

findings.line: The rest could wait until the horses found rhythm.
findings.reason: This paragraph closes by narrating pacing discipline instead of ending on the urgent exchange itself.
findings.suggestedFix: The horses found rhythm under them.

audits.dominantPattern: P5/P6 sweep — scenery with posture or motion verbs

findings.line: The real seat sat on the island.
findings.pattern: P5 sweep — architecture with posture verb
findings.reason: Architecture is given bodily posture; the concrete fact is simply that the island holds the real stronghold.
findings.suggestedFix: The real stronghold was on the island.

findings.line: Jaina's trail did not go up.
findings.pattern: P5 sweep — trail with motion verb
findings.reason: The trail is made to choose a direction; the real agents are the drips, chalk dust, and scuffs pointing lower.
findings.suggestedFix: The drips, chalk dust, and scuffs all led down.

findings.line: The clean room below would sit over the same butcher's work.
findings.pattern: P6/P5 sweep — room with posture verb
findings.reason: The room is given bodily posture instead of naming the physical layout above the butchered crypt rooms.
findings.suggestedFix: The clean room below would be built over the same butcher's work.

findings.line: The sanctum answered with a pulse sharp enough to shake the jars.
findings.reason: The room is made to answer; the real agent is the wardwork discharging through the floor.
findings.suggestedFix: A pulse ripped through the wardwork hard enough to shake the jars.

findings.line: Ahead the south road ran through contaminated dark toward a city whose people might already be eating their own dead.
findings.pattern: P5 sweep — geography with motion verb
findings.reason: The road is given motion in the dedicated sweep sense; the concrete fact is the route cutting through contaminated dark.
findings.suggestedFix: Ahead, the south road cut through contaminated dark toward a city whose people might already be eating their own dead.

audits.registerSummary: Sylvanas thinks like a field commander: clipped, tactical, route-first, and alert to posture, exits, threat, and competence. She will use hard labels when they help a decision in progress, but she does not naturally pause for thematic captions or polished summaries from above the moment.
audits.structuralHabit: yes — caption fragments recur at threshold and transition beats.

findings.line: Automatic, and still built for four.
findings.povMismatch: The fragment captions grief geometry from above instead of staying on Cyndia's visible motion and Sylvanas's practical read.
findings.suggestedFix: Cyndia wrapped the oarlocks for a four-woman boat.

findings.line: Then the desk.
findings.povMismatch: This is a cinematic cut caption, not a thought Sylvanas needs to phrase.
findings.suggestedFix: Her eyes dropped to the desk.

findings.line: She had come down through butcher's waste to find refinement laid over it and Jaina standing in the middle with the settled posture of someone already inside the work.
findings.povMismatch: The sentence is more polished and thematically packaged than Sylvanas's usual threat-and-posture register in the instant of entry.
findings.suggestedFix: She had come down through butcher's waste and found Jaina standing in the clean center of it.

findings.line: Escort-close.
findings.povMismatch: The fragment works as a caption pinned over the spacing instead of as a live in-body thought.
findings.suggestedFix: She kept escort distance.

findings.line: Alive.
findings.povMismatch: A single-word verdict after the sleeve-and-hem details reads like authorial captioning, not fresh perception.
findings.suggestedFix: Alive and on her feet.

findings.line: The scholar above had not lied. She had only told it through envy.
findings.deletionTest: She had only told it through envy.
findings.reason: Preamble negation frames the observation by denial before naming the real conclusion.
findings.suggestedFix: The scholar above had told it through envy.

findings.line: Sylvanas did not answer. Her eyes went to Jaina again.
findings.deletionTest: Her eyes went to Jaina again.
findings.reason: The first sentence primes absence so the real beat can arrive in the second.
findings.suggestedFix: Her eyes went to Jaina again.

findings.line: Jaina did not flinch from the backlash. She cut her staff through the air and the broken lines folded inward, narrower now, turning the room into a closing cage.
findings.deletionTest: She cut her staff through the air and the broken lines folded inward, narrower now, turning the room into a closing cage.
findings.reason: The negated reaction is scaffolding; the committed action already carries Jaina's steadiness.
findings.suggestedFix: She cut her staff through the air and the broken lines folded inward, narrower now, turning the room into a closing cage.

findings.line: Sylvanas did not go to her. She crossed to the desk instead and stood over Kel'Thuzad collapsed against the chair.
findings.deletionTest: She crossed to the desk instead and stood over Kel'Thuzad collapsed against the chair.
findings.reason: The negative setup exists only to sharpen the second sentence's choice.
findings.suggestedFix: She crossed to the desk instead and stood over Kel'Thuzad collapsed against the chair.

audits.cleanReason: Counted all capped tic families. There are 0 corner-of-mouth beats, 1 physical use of 'marked', 1 physical movement use of 'eased', 1 non-body atmospheric 'tightened', and 2 physical-position 'shifted' uses. No hard cap is exceeded, and none of the counted uses is the weakest generic version of its tic.

summary.verdictReason: 28 KILL verdicts across 4 guides. Worst concentration: GPT-5 F4/F1 around the sanctum read and aftermath; narrator-seep A1 verdict fragments recur through transitions.
